positioning: Use the knowledge tree as the review backbone. Example problems and future wrong questions both feed the same review state.

field_specs.node_id: Leaf node id in the knowledge tree
field_specs.stability: Positive memory stability value for forgetting scheduling
field_specs.linked_question_ids: Example or wrong-question ids linked to this leaf
field_specs.source_batch_ids: Which example batch or wrong-question batch introduced this node
field_specs.first_seen_at: When this node entered the student's review system
field_specs.correct_count: Correct review count on this node
field_specs.wrong_count: Wrong review count on this node
field_specs.priority_note: Short human-readable reason for current priority

field_specs.linked_node_ids: All linked leaf nodes used by review scheduling
field_specs.source_batch_id: Which batch this question came from
field_specs.review_count: How many times the question has been revisited
field_specs.priority_note: Why this question should be shown now

field_specs.recommended_question_ids: Example or wrong-question ids to revisit first
field_specs.recommended_bundles: Small mixed bundles such as one leaf plus one representative question

simple_scheduler_rules: New knowledge points should be reviewed before they are considered stable
Each newly introduced knowledge point should have at least one representative example question
If a question is answered wrong, raise the priority of all linked knowledge points
If a knowledge point remains unstable, prioritize leaf-card review before pushing more similar questions
A daily plan can mix knowledge-point review and question review, but should stay lightweight
